content.full_text:
FPPC
Nov 14 9 50 AM '90

November 9, 1990


1101- 2nd Street
Hermosa Beach, California 90254

SCOTT HALLABRIN
ACTING GENERAL COUNSEL
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

RE: My Request for Informal Assistance
     Your File No.: I-90-563

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

The purpose of this letter is to once again inquire as to the status of the above referenced request for assistance regarding a rent/gift issue.

I originally wrote to you requesting advise on or about August 27, 1990. You wrote me acknowledging receipt on September 7, 1990, and assigned it the above reference number. On September 17, 1990 I wrote you once again with a few additional questions on this same issue. I never received acknowledgment of my second letter. On October 10, 1990 I again wrote regarding the status of this issue, and enclosed a copy of my second letter since it had never been acknowledged.

On October 12, 1990, you corresponded to me and gave me some guidelines regarding this issue. I appreciate your assistance in taking the time to provide information on this matter.

However, I still have questions that have not been addressed or answered. I would appreciate it if you would acknowledge my second letter and questions and let me know if you are going to be responding to them.

Additionally, in your letter of October 12, it begins by qualifying that the letter is only informal assistance since my letter does not mention a specific governmental decision. This is a bit confusing to me because in the letter you reference an earlier advise letter to Charles Vose, City Attorney No. 1-90-415, which was regarding a specific zoning issue. That is what started this whole matter. He had requested if I had a potential conflict due to living within 300 feet of a property that was before the Council for rezoning as I was a month to month tenant, and not an owner. You responded that I did not, and brought up this "gift" situation.

SCOTT HALLABRIN
Page Two
November 9, 1990

In summary, the following are the outstanding questions which I feel are
relevant to this issue and would appreciate your consideration of a further
response.

Does this potential gift situation exist if I were a lessee?

When did your determination take affect that low rent is a
"thing of value" and therefore a "gift"? Has it been in affect
since the Political Reform Act was passed by the voters in
1974?

Would this kind of "gift" be required to be reported under the
City of Hermosa Beach Conflict of Interest Code, and the
applicable disclosure categories?

Can my length of tenancy be taken into consideration in compar-
ison to the "fair market value"?

I am enclosing copies of my previous letters for reference.

Thank you for your courtesy and cooperation.

Sincerely,

Kathleen Midstokke

KATHLEEN L. MIDSTOKKE

sections.question_synthetic: The requester seeks clarification on whether a potential gift situation exists if they were a lessee rather than an owner, when low rent was determined to be a 'thing of value' as a gift, whether such gifts must be reported under Hermosa Beach's Conflict of Interest Code, and whether length of tenancy affects comparison to fair market value.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. The requester is following up on a previous informal advice letter (October 12, 1990) and seeking further clarification on four specific legal questions regarding gifts and conflict of interest disclosure requirements. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present in this document.

embedding.summary: This is a follow-up letter from Kathleen L. Midstokke to FPPC Acting General Counsel Scott Hallabrin requesting acknowledgment and further response to outstanding questions regarding a potential gift/conflict of interest situation involving low rent on leased property, originally submitted in August 1990 (File No. I-90-563).
